title: selinum cicutarium
intro: Selinum cicutarium, also known as wild carrot or queen anne's lace, is a biennial plant in the parsley family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Selinum cicutarium include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make poultices for external use or taken internally to promote overall health and well-being.
